cards.title: Google Vids Adds Personalized AI Avatars
cards.body: Google is expanding Vids with personalized AI avatars that let users star in their own videos. The feature integrates Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

cards.title: Roblox Launches AI Game Creation Feature
cards.body: Roblox's new 'Build' feature lets users generate basic games with a single text prompt on mobile. The tool democratizes game creation for developers of all skill levels.

cards.title: Google AI Mode Now Links to Apps
cards.body: Google's AI Mode is expanding beyond Q&A to complete tasks across users' regularly-used apps. The update marks a shift toward agentic AI capabilities for everyday workflows.

cards.title: DoorDash Launches Command-Line Tool
cards.body: DoorDash opened a limited beta of dd-cli, allowing developers and AI agents to search stores and place orders from the terminal. The tool represents a shift toward AI-agent-first software design.

cards.title: OpenAI Releases First Hardware Product
cards.body: OpenAI launched its first hardware offering this week, including a ChatGPT-branded basketball. The move signals the company's expansion beyond software into physical products.

cards.title: Former DeepMind Researcher Raises $300M
cards.body: A former DeepMind researcher launched a startup at a $300M pre-seed valuation, leveraging a decade of experience building influential AI systems. The founder believes visual AI is a major frontier in artificial intelligence.

cards.title: AMI Labs CEO Dismisses 'AGI' Label
cards.body: Alexandre LeBrun, CEO of Yann LeCun's world model startup AMI Labs, rejects the term 'superintelligence' despite industry trends. The company focuses on practical AI development rather than speculative terminology.

cards.title: Moonshot's Kimi 3 Challenges Anthropic
cards.body: Moonshot's upcoming Kimi K3 is expected to narrow the gap with Anthropic's Opus 4.8 model. The 2-3 trillion parameter model will be China's largest open AI model.

cards.title: Apple Intelligence Launches in China
cards.body: Apple secured deals with Alibaba and Baidu to power Apple Intelligence in China. The partnership marks a crucial step for Apple's AI strategy in the key market.

cards.title: Applied Computing Raises $20M Series A
cards.body: Applied Computing closed a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for oil, gas, and petrochemical industries. The vertical-specific model aims to optimize entire plant operations.

cards.title: Microsoft Trains Sales on AI Competitors
cards.body: Microsoft is coaching salespeople to position its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective alternatives. The strategy targets OpenAI and Anthropic's market dominance.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches $230 Coding Keyboard
cards.body: OpenAI released a light-up keyboard designed for its agentic coding app Codex amid a legal battle with Apple. The hardware move expands OpenAI's product portfolio.

cards.title: Thinking Machines Releases Inkling Model
cards.body: Thinking Machines launched Inkling, its first open model, challenging one-size-fits-all AI approaches. The release marks the company's first public proof point after 18 months of infrastructure development.

cards.title: Hack Reveals Suno Scraped YouTube
cards.body: A hacker accessed Suno's source code using stolen employee credentials, exposing how the AI music generator scraped decades of YouTube audio. The discovery raises training data transparency questions.

cards.title: Whatnot Acquires Shaped for Recommendations
cards.body: Livestream shopping platform Whatnot acquired AI startup Shaped to power real-time recommendations. The deal enhances personalization and discovery features across new product categories.

cards.title: Microsoft Patches Record 570 Vulnerabilities
cards.body: Microsoft's latest Patch Tuesday resolved a record 570 security vulnerabilities using AI-powered discovery. The company credits AI tools for accelerating vulnerability identification and fixes.

cards.title: Anthropic Launches Enterprise Services Venture
cards.body: Ode with Anthropic is a joint venture deploying forward-deployed engineers in enterprise firms with backing from Blackstone and Goldman Sachs. The model bets that small engineering teams can replace traditional consulting armies.

cards.title: Google Vids Adds AI Avatars for Personalized Video Creation
cards.body: Google is integrating personalized AI avatars into Vids, letting users create videos starring digital versions of themselves. The feature combines Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

cards.title: Roblox Launches AI-Powered Game Creation Tool
cards.body: Roblox's new "Build" feature enables users to generate basic games using a single text prompt. This AI-driven tool democratizes game creation for mobile app users.

cards.title: Google AI Mode Expands to Task Completion Across Apps
cards.body: Google's AI Mode now allows users to link and interact with select apps, extending beyond question-answering. The update enables AI to complete tasks across apps users regularly rely on.

cards.title: DoorDash Opens Beta for AI-Friendly Command-Line Tool
cards.body: DoorDash's dd-cli lets developers and AI agents search stores, build carts, and place orders from the terminal. This marks a shift toward software designed for AI agents alongside human users.

cards.title: DeepMind Researcher Launches Visual AI Startup at $300M
cards.body: Former DeepMind researcher Andrew Dai raised a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching his product, drawing on a decade building influential AI systems including ChatGPT research. He believes visual AI represents the next major AI frontier.

cards.title: AMI Labs CEO Rejects "AGI" and "Superintelligence" Terminology
cards.body: Alexandre LeBrun, CEO of Yann LeCun's world model startup AMI Labs, dismisses industry terminology around superintelligence. While the AI field chases AGI, LeBrun takes a different philosophical stance.

cards.title: China's Moonshot Kimi 3 Expected to Rival Anthropic's Opus
cards.body: Moonshot's upcoming Kimi K3 will be China's largest open AI model, with 2-3 trillion parameters. The model is expected to close the gap with Anthropic's advanced Opus 4.8.

cards.title: Apple Intelligence Approved in China with Alibaba, Baidu
cards.body: Apple secured approval to launch Apple Intelligence in China through partnerships with Alibaba and Baidu. This deal marks a crucial step for Apple's AI ambitions in a key market.

cards.title: Applied Computing Raises $20M for Oil & Gas AI Model
cards.body: Applied Computing secured $20M in Series A funding to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas, and petrochemical industries. The model aims to manage entire plant operations.

cards.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-Red: AI Hacker for Model Security
cards.body: OpenAI built GPT-Red, an LLM super-hacker designed to stress-test and improve model defenses against cyberattacks. The latest GPT-5.6 was trained against GPT-Red to enhance security.

cards.title: Microsoft Trains Salespeople to Downplay Competitors' AI
cards.body: Microsoft is coaching salespeople to position its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than OpenAI and Anthropic alternatives. The strategy reflects intensifying competition in enterprise AI.

cards.title: OpenAI Releases $230 Light-Up Keyboard for Codex
cards.body: OpenAI launched a specialized light-up keyboard designed for its agentic coding app Codex. The hardware release comes amid legal battles with Apple over alleged trade theft.

cards.title: Thinking Machines Launches Inkling, First Open AI Model
cards.body: Thinking Machines released Inkling, its first public open model, after 18 months of private AI infrastructure development. The launch represents the company's bet against one-size-fits-all AI approaches.

cards.title: Suno Hack Reveals YouTube Scraping for AI Music Training
cards.body: A hacker accessed Suno's source code using employee credentials, revealing the music generator scraped decades of YouTube audio for training. The discovery raises questions about data sourcing practices.

cards.title: Whatnot Acquires Shaped for Real-Time AI Recommendations
cards.body: Whatnot acquired Shaped, a machine learning company specializing in real-time recommendations and search. The acquisition will enhance Whatnot's personalization and discovery features across product categories.

cards.title: Microsoft Patches Record 570 Vulnerabilities Using AI
cards.body: Microsoft's latest Patch Tuesday resolved a record 570 security vulnerabilities across its product line. AI-powered discovery tools significantly accelerated the identification process.

cards.title: Ode with Anthropic: AI Services as Enterprise Future
cards.body: Ode with Anthropic is a joint venture embedding forward-deployed engineers into enterprise firms, backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, and Goldman Sachs. The bet: a small AI-enabled team can replace traditional consulting armies.

cards.title: Anthropic Discovers Hidden Conceptual Space in Claude AI
cards.body: Anthropic researchers identified a hidden computational space where Claude processes complex concepts, offering the clearest view yet into AI model internals. The discovery provides new insights into how large language models actually work.
